# The Arecibo Message

## Overview

The Arecibo message is a radio broadcast aimed at a distant globular star cluster in 1974 as a demonstration of human technical achievement and a symbolic greeting to any listeners far away.

## Who chose what it carried

The transmission was overseen by a small committee of scientists who spent weeks arguing over what a curated portrait of an entire species should contain. They selected and arranged everything that went into it: numeric greetings encoded in binary, the recorded structure of human DNA, figures of music and mathematics drawn from human culture, and a long sequence of encoded diagrams depicting people, the solar system, and the chemistry of life. Every inclusion was debated, because the committee knew the broadcast might be the only trace of them that ever reached another world.
